title: AT&T, T-Mobile and Verizon form D2D JV for rural coverage
content: AT&T, T-Mobile and Verizon agree to create a satellite D2D joint venture to fill coverage dead zones and simplify integration with more satellite providers. But plenty of details are still lacking.

title: Asia Link Cable completes landing in Hong Kong in major milestone
content: Asia Link Cable has successfully landed at the Chung Hom Kok Cable Landing Station in Hong Kong. The station is China Telecom's first self-built submarine cable landing station outside mainland China.

title: SpaceX is now a spectrum holder, not just a satellite operator
content: What the EchoStar deals actually change: AT&T adds rural lowband coverage it's lacked since 2008 and SpaceX graduates from T-Mobile vendor to spectrum holder.

title: EchoStar unlikely to challenge FCC's $2.4B escrow condition – analyst
content: EchoStar is mulling its next move after the FCC approved the company's spectrum sales to AT&T and SpaceX with a $2.5 billion escrow condition, but New Street Research's Blair Levin says it's unlikely EchoStar will contest the decision.
